name: Market Chameleon
description_short: Market Chameleon is a specialized stock and options analytics platform with advanced IV rankings, earnings strategy backtesting, and unusual options flow screening for active traders.
description_long:
Market Chameleon is a web-based trading analytics platform founded in 2006, built for active stock and options traders who need deep, data-driven insights beyond what typical screeners provide. The platform covers stock data back to 1998 and options data back to 2014, offering a rare combination of historical depth and actionable real-time analytics — all accessible from a browser with no software installation required.

The platform's core strength lies in its options analytics suite. Traders can access implied volatility rankings, historical IV percentiles, unusual options volume, block trade screening, and repeat trade detection. Multi-leg strategy backtesting — covering iron condors, verticals, and butterflies — lets traders validate approaches against real historical data rather than theoretical models, making it a serious research tool for options-focused traders.

Market Chameleon's earnings tools are its standout differentiator. The Earnings Trader plan allows users to backtest options strategies across up to 12 past earnings releases, comparing implied moves against actual historical moves and filtering by win rate and average return. This level of earnings-specific analysis is rare among retail-facing analytics platforms and gives options traders a meaningful statistical edge around catalysts.

Stock tools include a premarket and postmarket analytics suite with intraday volume data, VWAP, and trades-per-minute breakdowns, plus a stock screener covering sector, market cap, and 52-week range filters. Pricing starts at $39/month for the Stock Trader plan, rising to $99/month for Total Access which unlocks all plan features and unlimited data exports. A 7-day free trial is available on all premium plans, and the platform is also accessible via Interactive Brokers third-party research subscriptions.

tiers.name: Options Trader
features: Unusual options flow and order flow sentiment
Block Trade and Repeat Trade screeners
Implied volatility rankings and historical IV back to 2014

tiers.name: Earnings Trader
features: Earnings strategy backtesting across 12 past releases
Implied vs actual historical move comparisons
Filter by win rate and average return

pros: Best-in-class earnings strategy backtesting across up to 12 real historical releases
Deep implied volatility data and IV rankings back to 2014
Unusual options flow and block trade screening for order flow analysis
Granular premarket and postmarket analytics with VWAP and volume breakdowns
Available through Interactive Brokers for streamlined subscription access
cons: No mobile app — browser-only platform limits on-the-go access
Options and earnings tools split across separate paid plans requiring upgrades
Interface can feel dense and overwhelming for beginners
Annual pricing not clearly advertised on the website
